2603.20993.title: Long-Term Outlier Prediction Through Outlier Score Modeling
2603.20993.paper_summary: This study addresses an important gap in time series outlier detection by proposing a novel problem setting: long-term outlier prediction. Conventional methods primarily focus on immediate detection by identifying deviations from normal patterns. As a result, their applicability is limited when forecasting outlier events far into the future. To overcome this limitation, we propose a simple and unsupervised two-layer method that is independent of specific models. The first layer performs standard outlier detection, and the second layer predicts future outlier scores based on the temporal structure of previously observed outliers. This framework enables not only pointwise detection but also long-term forecasting of outlier likelihoods. Experiments on synthetic datasets show that the proposed method performs well in both detection and prediction tasks. These findings suggest that the method can serve as a strong baseline for future work in outlier detection and forecasting.
2603.20993.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究針對時間序列異常檢測中的一個重要空白，提出了一個新穎的問題設定：長期異常預測。傳統方法主要集中於通過識別與正常模式的偏差來進行即時檢測。因此，當預測未來遙遠的異常事件時，它們的適用性受到限制。為了克服這一限制，我們提出了一種簡單且無監督的兩層方法，該方法不依賴於特定模型。第一層執行標準的異常檢測，第二層根據先前觀察到的異常的時間結構預測未來的異常分數。這個框架不僅能進行逐點檢測，還能長期預測異常的可能性。在合成數據集上的實驗顯示，所提出的方法在檢測和預測任務中表現良好。這些發現表明，該方法可以作為未來異常檢測和預測工作的強基準。
2603.20993.author: Yuma Aoki et.al.
2603.20993.authors: Yuma Aoki, Joon Park, Koh Takeuchi, Hisashi Kashima, Shinya Akimoto, Ryuichi Hashimoto, Takahiro Adachi, Takeshi Kishikawa, Takamitsu Sasaki
